summary: A Russian Ka-52 helicopter was downed near Nadiivka, Donetsk region, with FPV drones striking the crew during their evacuation attempt. A rare Ukrainian fixed-wing drone, the Klin, was intercepted and destroyed; it carried up to 5 kg of payload, had a 120 km range, and featured a modern Li-AFB battery. The Elka anti-drone system performed multiple kinetic interceptions of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, including the Darts model, causing onboard charges to detonate. On March 21 and 23, 2026, the Rubikon Center reported eliminating 83 and 94 targets respectively, including Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and control points across the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions.
